__fixture__.name: onboarding_state_recent_work_metadata_device_scoped
__fixture__.scenario: The state item state_item.recent_work_metadata is intentionally NOT portable: recent-work rows reference workspace identifiers whose leak across devices would be both a privacy exposure and a UX confusion (a row pointing at /Users/alice/projects/foo is meaningless on bob's device). state_portability_class = device_scoped, reset_class = resettable_per_device, export_class = not_exported_machine_local, profile_scope_class = per_device. Exercises §6.2 rule 3 (device_scoped ↔ per_device), §3.6 rule 4 (device_scoped + recent_work ↔ not_exported_machine_local), and §7.2.9 (recent_work_metadata_is_device_scoped).

portability_reason: Recent-work rows reference workspace identifiers whose meaning is device-local; carrying them to a new device would either leak identifiers or point at paths that do not exist. The user clears per-device via the Clear recent work control.
